strengths: Proven automation and ETL pipeline delivery
BI tooling: Power BI and Tableau
Quantified business impact (e.g., revenue lift, efficiency gains)
weaknesses: Only 2+ years total; borderline for senior expectations
No explicit experience with Azure-specific services
improvement_bullets: Architected robust ELT pipelines using dbt and Snowflake to transform raw ticket sales data into actionable business models, ensuring high data quality for team marketing stakeholders.

Collaborated with business analysts to develop, document, and standardize data structures, bridging the gap between raw cloud-based data and the specific requirements of marketing and ticketing operations.
Streamlined analytical reporting workflows by integrating Azure/Snowflake environments with visualization tools, reducing time-to-insight for leadership teams.
Applied statistical modeling and data science techniques to evaluate fan engagement and ticketing performance, delivering insights that directly influence team business strategy.

Consulted with internal departments to translate business requirements into technical specifications, ensuring that data infrastructure decisions align with long-term organizational financial priorities.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Team Data Scientist opportunity with the National Basketball Association (NBA). I am interested in this opportunity because of the unique intersection of high-stakes sports business and data-driven decision-making. What interests me about working for the NBA is its approach to global fan connectivity and digital innovation, which excites me because of the league’s commitment to evolving the fan journey through data. I believe my expertise in building scalable ETL pipelines and my proficiency in predictive modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached complex data problems by focusing on automating inefficient workflows and standardizing organizational KPIs. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a need to move away from manual reporting; I engineered a Python-based automation pipeline that integrated CRM and POS data, which saved 8 hours of manual entry per week and improved reporting accuracy. This allowed the leadership team to make faster, more informed decisions on marketing spend, resulting in a 300% increase in revenue for targeted customer segments. Ultimately, I know that the Team Data Scientist role at the NBA would allow me to bring my analytical rigor to key business lines like ticketing and marketing. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my stakeholder management skills. I regularly translated complex technical findings into actionable business recommendations for cross-functional partners, ensuring our data governance frameworks were always aligned with our primary financial objectives. This experience in closing the gap between raw data and growth strategy is exactly what I plan to bring to your TMBO partnerships. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python, SQL, and dbt to optimize third-party data streams and enhance reporting products at the NBA. I know that my technical proficiency in cloud environments and my commitment to business-aligned analytics align with what it takes to succeed at the Team Data Scientist role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
